note: Ladder rung 2: GDSF (Cherkasova 1998), the first cost-consulting policy. Under the linear cost model it reduces to LFU-with-aging, so this row measures what the FREQUENCY signal alone collects of LRU's 60-point room over the oracle. Answer: negative, and the canonical version's reset-thrash mechanism was predicted and is confirmed by ablation.
reset_thrash_ablation: Canonical GDSF resets frequency per-residency; the retained-history variant keeps it across residency gaps. On this workload the canonical reset costs ~23 points (203.2 -> 180.5). Mechanism, as predicted: agentic re-reads bring an evicted hot block straight back as a freq-1 stranger, making it the next cheapest victim (a self-reinforcing thrash loop for exactly the hot blocks). Retained history lets the block re-enter with standing, breaking the loop. The prediction was recorded in the GDSF docstring before the run; the ablation confirms it.
finding: Even retained-history GDSF (180.5%) loses to LRU (160.5%): the frequency signal, with or without history, is worse than pure recency on agentic re-reads. Cumulative ladder result so far: no simple single-signal heuristic beats LRU. Recency 160.5% < idle-time (IdleTTL) 176.4% < frequency-with-history 180.5% < frequency-reset 203.2%. The 60 points of room over the oracle are collected by NONE of the classic heuristics; they make it worse. This sharpens the case that lifecycle/workflow information (WA-LRU, retired-cache, gap-aware protection, the economic policy) is what the room requires.
deeper_mechanism: Why frequency loses to recency here, one level down (and it predicts the next rows): append-only prefix re-reads mean every resident block is touched every turn, so frequency is nearly age-in-turns, i.e. frequency ~= recency. The one thing frequency ADDS over recency is a bias recency lacks: old blocks accumulate unbeatable counts, so LFU-with-aging over-protects the EARLY prefix relative to fresh-but-hot recent context. On a workload where compaction is what kills old blocks, over-protecting antiquity is exactly wrong. Prediction this makes: WA-LRU (recency + reuse-probability + size) should beat plain frequency for the same reason, and any policy that distinguishes DEAD from OLD (the lifecycle rungs) is attacking the component of the 60 points that neither recency nor frequency can see.
scoreboard: Three policy families in, zero have beaten the floor (LRU 160.5%). This is the pre-registered hard mode materializing, not a disappointing interim: the room provably exists (the oracle collects it) and the cheap signals provably do not reach it. By elimination, the 60 points live in information LRU lacks: which blocks are dead (lifecycle) and what blocks cost (economics, once the cost term stops being vestigial under real/ swept cost figures).
performance: GDSF ran the high-pressure cell in 24.6s, under the ~29s budget; the per-eviction sort survives at 256 granularity, so the heap optimization is again declined (measured, not assumed). Receipt: LRU 17.7s, IdleTTL 13.9s, GDSF 24.6s, all under budget.
